signals.headline: SEC CLARITY Act Roundtable Scheduled | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
SEC CLARITY Act Roundtable Scheduled | YES | HIGH confidence**
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is holding a CLARITY Act roundtable discussion on April 16, which could formally classify XRP as a digital commodity[2]. The legislation carries 72% odds on Polymarket and could unlock an estimated $4-8 billion in dedicated XRP ETF capital if passed[3].

signals.headline: Solana DeFi Recovery Despite Drift Protocol Hack | MIXED | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana DeFi Recovery Despite Drift Protocol Hack | MIXED | MED confidence**
Tether announced a $150 million recovery plan for Drift Protocol users after an April 1 exploit drained $285 million[5]. While damaging to confidence, the swift large-scale recovery effort demonstrates ecosystem support[5].

signals.headline: Solana ETF Inflows Accelerate | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana ETF Inflows Accelerate | YES | HIGH confidence**
U.S. spot Solana ETFs recorded $5.04 million in net inflows on April 16, following a $5.36 million inflow the previous day, with cumulative net assets reaching $835 million[5]. This signals sustained institutional demand and validates Solana's price support.

signals.headline: XRP Weekly ETF Inflows Surge Past $119 Million | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP Weekly ETF Inflows Surge Past $119 Million | YES | HIGH confidence**
Weekly XRP ETF inflows hit $119.6 million, accounting for over half of all global crypto fund inflows for the week[1]. This demonstrates substantial institutional capital rotation into XRP.

signals.headline: Crypto Market Rally on Iran Peace Signals | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Crypto Market Rally on Iran Peace Signals | YES | HIGH confidence**
Bitcoin broke above $75,000 after Iran signaled it wants to resume peace talks with the United States[1]. Bitcoin climbed 5.9%, Ethereum rallied 8.6%, XRP gained 4.2%, and Solana rose 6.3%[1].

signals.raw:
South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026 (from 2.0% prior), below market forecasts of 2.4% | NO | HIGH confidence**  
This March figure, released around early April 2026 per Trading Economics data, marks the latest confirmed reading and stayed near the Bank of Korea's 2% target amid global energy volatility from the Iran/Middle East conflict[1][4][5].
